summary: Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 124 hostile drones across the north, south and east, while Russian forces halted rail traffic between Kerch and Simferopol after a drone attack. Multiple Shahed‑type UAVs were launched from Russian regions toward Ukrainian oblasts including Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, causing infrastructure damage and civilian casualties. Zelensky said he met Russian oligarch Abramovich and reiterated Ukraine will not withdraw from Donbas, emphasizing any compromise must follow a ceasefire.
key_points: Ukrainian air defence downed 124 enemy UAVs of various types during the night of 7‑8 June, with about ten drones still airborne (Source: @kpszsu/64426).
Rail traffic on the Kerch‑Simferopol line was stopped after a drone attack, halting at least ten trains (Source: @tass_agency/379740).

Shahed and other strike drones were launched from Russian regions including Bryansk, Kursk, Orel, and Crimea toward Ukrainian territories such as Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/64426).

A drone hit a Ukrposhta hub in Kharkiv, partially destroying it but causing no fatalities; other drone strikes damaged energy infrastructure in Odesa and injured civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81759).
Zelensky met Russian oligarch Abramovich in Kyiv and said Ukraine will not leave Donbas, insisting any compromise follows a ceasefire (Source: @UkraineNow/81756).
Two Shahed missiles were reported over Luhansk region (Lublin/Pyriatyn/Grebinka) (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67523).
Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel for the first time since April, with Israel claiming to have intercepted all targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81757).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defence downed 124 hostile drones across multiple fronts, while Russian forces halted rail traffic on the Kerch‑Simferopol line after a drone attack. Shahed‑type UAVs were launched from Russian territories toward Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, causing infrastructure damage and civilian injuries. Zelensky reiterated Ukraine will not withdraw from Donbas despite a meeting with Russian oligarch Abramovich.

points: Ukrainian air defence reported shooting down 124 enemy UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and Bandera types, across the north, south and east on the night of 7‑8 June, with remnants falling on 6 locations (Source: @kpszsu/64426).

A drone struck the Ukrposhta hub in Kharkiv, partially destroying the facility but leaving people unharmed; other UAV attacks damaged an energy object in southern Odesa region, causing power outages for over a thousand consumers (Source: @UkraineNow/81759).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV hit residential buildings in Konotop, Sumy region, with three injured and ongoing rescue operations in a five‑storey building (Source: @UkraineNow/81759).
Zelensky met Russian oligarch Abramovich in Kyiv, stating Ukraine will not withdraw from Donbas and any compromise must follow a ceasefire (Source: @UkraineNow/81756).
Ukrainian authorities noted drone activity from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, Chornomorsk, and Liman (Source: @kpszsu/64419).
Ukrainian air alerts were issued over Kyiv at 00:51 on 8 June (Source: @eRadarrua/95894).
Ukrainian forces reported a group of UAVs from Bryansk region heading toward northern Sumy oblast (Source: @kpszsu/64399).
Russian authorities halted rail traffic on the Kerch‑Simferopol line after a UAV attack, stopping at least ten trains (Source: @tass_agency/379740).
The Russian Ministry of Defense claimed 310 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian regions during the night (Source: @tass_agency/379734).
Six UAVs were destroyed over Tula region, according to the regional governor (Source: @tass_agency/379729).
A UAV attack caused a fire at a facility in Novorossiysk, with no casualties reported (Source: @tass_agency/379735).
Russian security services reported the disruption of an online‑casino financing network (Source: @tass_agency/379736).
Russian authorities announced a four‑day work week for June 8‑11 in celebration of Russia Day (Source: @tass_agency/379746).
Russian officials reported the capture of 24 members of an organized crime group linked to online‑casino financing (Source: @tass_agency/379742).
UAVs launched from Russian Bryansk, Kursk, Orel, and Crimea regions were directed toward Sumy, Kharkiv, and Donetsk areas, with 20 strike drones hitting 17 locations (Source: @kpszsu/64426).
A group of UAVs from the north of Kharkiv region moved along the Belarus border toward Zhytomyr region (Source: @kpszsu/64404).
UAVs from the Black Sea were reported heading to Odesa, Chornomorsk, and Liman in the Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/64419).
Two Shahed missiles were reported over Luhansk oblast, targeting Lublin, Pyriatyn, and Grebinka (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67523).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted UAVs over the eastern Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/64409).
UAV activity from the north of Kherson region moved toward Dnipropetrovsk, targeting Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/64403).
UAVs from the north of Kharkiv region were observed heading toward Sumy, Okhtyrka, Shostka, and Krolevets (Source: @kpszsu/64420).
UAVs from Bryansk region were directed toward northern Sumy oblast (Source: @kpszsu/64399).
A group of UAVs on the north of Kyiv region moved along the Belarus border toward Zhytomyr (Source: @kpszsu/64404).
UAVs from the north of Zhytomyr region moved westward along the Belarus border (Source: @kpszsu/64408).
UAVs from Chernihiv region were reported heading toward Kyiv region, specifically Vyshedor district (Source: @kpszsu/64431).
UAVs from the north of Kherson region were noted moving northward (Source: @kpszsu/64422).
UAVs from the north of Kharkiv region were observed heading toward Sumy and surrounding towns (Source: @kpszsu/64420).
UAVs from the north of Kharkiv region were also reported heading toward Boroduhiv and Vilshany (Source: @eRadarrua/95882).
UAV attacks in Zaporizhzhia caused infrastructure damage and a fire, with casualties reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
UAVs from the south of Sumy region were heading toward Kirikivka, Boromlya, and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/64425).
UAVs from the south of Kherson region moved toward Dnipropetrovsk, targeting Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/64403).
UAVs from the Black Sea headed toward Mykolaiv region, targeting Berezanka (Source: @kpszsu/64417).
A drone hit a residential building in Chornomorsk with no casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81759).
UAVs from the north of Kharkiv region were observed heading toward Chuhuiv and Velykyi Burluk in Kharkiv oblast (Source: @kpszsu/64425).
UAVs from the south of Kharkiv region were reported over the eastern part of the oblast (Source: @kpszsu/64409).
Ukrainian air defence downed 124 hostile UAVs of various types on the night of 7‑8 June (Source: @kpszsu/64426).
Russian authorities claimed 310 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian territory during the same night (Source: @tass_agency/379734).
Six UAVs were destroyed over Tula region (Source: @tass_agency/379729).
A UAV attack halted rail traffic on the Kerch‑Simferopol line (Source: @tass_agency/379740).
UAV attacks caused fires in Novorossiysk and other Russian facilities (Source: @tass_agency/379735).
UAVs from multiple Russian regions were launched toward Ukrainian targets, with 20 strike drones hitting 17 locations (Source: @kpszsu/64426).
Ukrainian air alerts were issued over Kyiv at 00:51 on 8 June (Source: @eRadarrua/95894).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defence reported shooting down 124 hostile UAVs of Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, Bandera and other types across the north, south and east on 7‑8 June (Source: @kpszsu/64426).
Rail traffic on the Kerch‑Simferopol line was halted after a drone attack, stopping at least ten trains (Source: @tass_agency/379740).

Shahed‑type strike drones were launched from Russian regions including Bryansk, Kursk, Orel and Crimea toward Ukrainian oblasts such as Sumy, Kharkiv and Donetsk, with 20 drones hitting 17 locations (Source: @kpszsu/64426).
A drone struck the Ukrposhta hub in Kharkiv, partially destroying it but causing no fatalities; other UAV attacks damaged energy infrastructure in Odesa region (Source: @UkraineNow/81759).
Zelensky met Russian oligarch Abramovich in Kyiv and reaffirmed Ukraine will not withdraw from Donbas, insisting any compromise follows a ceasefire (Source: @UkraineNow/81756).
Two Shahed missiles were reported over Luhansk region targeting Lublin, Pyriatyn and Grebinka (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67523).
Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel for the first time since April, with Israel claiming all missiles were intercepted (Source: @UkraineNow/81757).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian air defences are under sustained pressure from large UAV raids launched from multiple Russian regions, indicating a coordinated effort to degrade Ukrainian infrastructure and morale. The disruption of rail links in Crimea demonstrates Russia's vulnerability to UAV strikes on logistical nodes. Continued drone activity across all fronts, including southern and eastern axes, suggests the conflict remains highly kinetic with civilian infrastructure at risk.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defence assets and issuing frequent alerts, while also documenting civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Russian officials are emphasizing successful interceptions and attributing attacks to Ukrainian aggression, while maintaining operational control over contested transport routes. International observers note the escalation of UAV use as a key factor shaping the operational tempo on both sides.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch points in Russian oblasts such as Bryansk, Kursk and Orel is critical for early warning. The pattern of attacks on transport infrastructure, especially rail lines in Crimea, indicates a strategic focus on supply chain disruption. Continued diplomatic statements from Ukrainian leadership underscore a firm stance on territorial integrity, potentially influencing future negotiation dynamics.
